mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail and housing fails to predict positive quarterly returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in > 60% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates compresses real discount rates and corporate profit margins, forcing immediate equity valuation adjustments and negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Triggered periods show positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports sustained consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth series shows zero or negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or forward quarterly returns are uncorrelated with employment trends.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive equity market performance over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined GDP and retail strength fails to predict positive monthly returns (t-stat < 1.5) or signal reverses during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial output and deterioration in labor utilization signals an abrupt economic slowdown, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next week.
failure_condition: Triggered conditions are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
